LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS GARY WAI SHING WONG AND KENRY KWONG'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS PRESENT A SUFFICIENT SHOWING OF LIKELIHOOD OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS, AND HAVE DEMONSTRATED IRREPARABLE HARM IN THE FORECLOSURE OF THEIR HOME. PLAINTIFF SHALL POST A BOND IN THE AMOUNT OF $80,000 PLUS MAKE MONTHLY PAYMENTS IN THE AMOUNT OF $3,200.00 AS DETERMINED AT THIS HEARING, & PAYABLE STARTING 11/01/10. BOND TO BE POSTED BY THE END OF NOVEMBER. COUNSEL WAIVE NOTICE OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO DISSOLVE PRELIM INJUNCTION, AND MEMO IN SUPPORT THEREOF, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION FILED BY DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION HEARING SET FOR FEB-15-2011 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS WELLS FARGO BANK, NA, US BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THIS MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED. THE DEMURRER AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS UNJUST ENRICHMENT IS A FORM OF RESTITUTION NOT A VALID CAUSE OF ACTION. AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD THE UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICE WITH MORE PARTICULARITY (SEE BELOW AS TO 3RD, 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION). AS TO THE 3RD AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD MORE FACTS WITH SUFFICIENT PARTICULARITY AS TO WHO MADE THE MISREPRESENTATION AND WHEN. AS TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE ONLY REMEDY FOR WRONGFUL FORECLOSURE IS POSTPONEMENT BEFORE IT HAPPENS. MABREY V. SUPERIOR COURT (2010) 185 CAL.APP.4TH 208, 238. FURTHER, ONCE AN UNLAWFUL DETAINER JUDGMENT HAS BEEN RENDERED, SUBSEQUENT QUIET TITLE SUITS ARE BARRED. MALKOSKIE V. OPTION ONE MORTGAGE CORP. (2010) 188 CA 4TH 968, 974. THE COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFF 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. (302/LMG) JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-02-2011 Continued To Feb-17-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-26-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHAEL G. CROSS OF SEVERSON & WERSON, TEL. NO. 677-5617 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. AND U.S. BANK, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT - U.S. Bank's demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to all of the claims. Wells Fargo's demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the first, second and third claims and sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth claim. The order filed July 21, 2011 gave plaintiffs leave to plead the first three claims as to this defendant with more particularity yet plaintiffs failed to do so. Plaintiffs' allegations of agency, ratification, and conspiracy are conclusions of law, not required ultimate facts. The fourth claim is not permitted as to both demurring defendants because plaintiffs did not obtain leave to amend to plead that claim. In addition, Civil Code 2932.5 is inapposite. Plaintiffs are provided one last opportunity to plead further facts showing how Well's Fargo's conduct, including the request for the $40,859.00 to be placed in trust, was fraudulent, unfair, or unlawful. The current allegations do not clearly state how the payment was induced by fraud and is unfair and unlawful. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077 = (302/HEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-25-2011 Continued To May-20-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-01-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS WELLS FARGO BANK, NA, US BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Raymond Cho of Severson & Wwerson (398.3344) appearing via court call for defendants and moving parties; Auriol R. Steel of Consumer Protection Legal Services (800.991.7634) appearing via court call for plaintiff and responding party. This matter was not argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: sustained. The demurrer as to the 1st cause of action is sustained without leave to amend as unjust enrichment is a form of restitution not a valid cause of action. As to the 2nd cause of action, the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead the unfair business practice with more particularity (see below as to 3rd, 4th causes of action). As to the 3rd and 4th causes of action, the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead more facts with sufficient particularity as to who made the misrepresentation and when. As to the 5th cause of action, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The only remedy for wrongful foreclosure is postponement before it happens. Mabrey v. Superior Court (2010) 185 Cal.App.4th 208, 238. Further, once an unlawful detainer judgment has been rendered, subsequent quiet title suits are barred. Malkoskie v. Option One Mortgage Corp. (2010) 188 CA 4th 968, 974. The court grants plaintiff 20 days leave to amend the complaint. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

MacDonald (922.5939) for plaintiff and responding party; Raymond Cho of Severson & Werson (398.3344 x445) appearing telephonically for defendants Wells Fargo Bank and US Bank National Association. No other appearances. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Sustain with leave to amend. No opposition filed. Further, the court granted plaintiff's oral request for 30 days leave to amend. Mr. Cho to submit one proposed form of order as to this demurrer and the demurrer of Wells Fargo and US Bank. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

PLAINTIFFS GARY WAI SHING WONG AND KENRY KWONG'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION.

APPEARANCES: Sophia Andritsakis, Esq. 254-4446 specially appearing for Gary Lane, Esq. of Consumer Protection Legal Services, Inc. 800/991-7634 atty for pltfs & moving parties; Raymond Cho, Esq. of Severson & Werson 677-5569 for deft & opposing party Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified: GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS PRESENT A SUFFICIENT SHOWING OF LIKELIHOOD OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS, AND HAVE DEMONSTRATED IRREPARABLE HARM IN THE FORECLOSURE OF THEIR HOME. PLAINTIFF SHALL POST A BOND IN THE AMOUNT OF $80,000 PLUS MAKE MONTHLY PAYMENTS IN the AMOUNT of $3,200.00 as DETERMINED AT this HEARING, & payable starting 11/01/10. Bond to be posted by the end of November. Counsel waive notice of order. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
